tiny_practice.action: Pause and visualize God standing in the divine council, evaluating how justice is administered on earth

two_step.reflect_question: How can I actively work to defend the rights of the vulnerable in my community?

options.text: Volunteer with local social justice organizations
options.response: Research and commit to one local nonprofit serving marginalized groups

options.text: Speak up when I witness discrimination
options.response: Practice respectful, constructive intervention when witnessing unfair treatment

options.text: Support legal advocacy for the powerless
options.response: Donate to or support legal aid organizations that defend human rights

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a courtroom where God is the judge, with different figures representing the powerful and the weak

reevaluation.question: How has my understanding of divine justice shifted after reading this Psalm?
options: I now see justice as more active and participatory
I recognize my personal responsibility in defending the vulnerable
I understand God's perspective on social inequality differently
I feel more accountable for how power is used in society

three_choice.question: Which divine perspective resonates most in this psalm?

choices.expansion: Divine accountability transcends human political structures and exposes systemic injustice.
Temporal authority is ultimately fragile and subject to ultimate spiritual evaluation.

choices.text: The call to defend the weak
choices.expansion: True leadership is measured by protection of the marginalized and pursuit of justice.

socratic_nudge.question: If divine rulers are themselves subject to judgment, what standards should govern human governance?

moral_slider.question: How do human institutions balance power and justice?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of spiritual accountability shifted?
options: Saw interconnection between spiritual and political realms

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Justice and Accountability
thematic_summaries.summary: God stands in the divine council, evaluating earthly rulers and judges for their governance. He criticizes those who abuse power and fail to protect the vulnerable, demanding true justice for the marginalized.

thematic_summaries.title: God's Sovereignty Over Human Leadership
thematic_summaries.summary: Yahweh demonstrates ultimate authority by judging spiritual powers and human rulers who have been delegated responsibility. He reveals that all earthly leadership is ultimately accountable to divine standards.

reevaluation.question: How should we understand God's judgment of 'gods' in this passage?
options: Demonstrating God's supreme authority over all spiritual hierarchies
Symbolic representation of divine governance standards
